activated_rules.rule_name: Multiple documents in one PDF handled by selecting original certificate and corresponding invoice
Consignor found explicitly in invoice documents

consignor.invoice_value:
Postal address
Volvo Truck Corporation
S-405 08 Göteborg
Sweden
...
Volvo Truck Corporation in Belgium.
consignor.motivation: Certificate consignor name 'VOLVO TRUCK CORPORATION' is explicitly present in the invoice text on page 1 and page 2, including postal address block and exporter declaration. Country Sweden is explicitly present in the invoice postal address block. Under rule 4.1.0 this is sufficient even though the invoice also contains additional Volvo-related entities.

consignee.certificate_value:
MARUBENI DAGITIM VE SERVIS A.S
KUCUKBAKKALKOY MAH
KAYISDAGI CAD ALLIANZ PLAZA BLOK N1
34750 ISTANBUL
Türkiye
consignee.invoice_value:
Customer:
Marubeni Dagitim Ve Servis A.S
Kucukbakkalkoy Mah.
Kayisdagi Cad. Allianz Plaza Blok N
34750 ISTANBUL
Turkey
consignee.motivation: The consignee named in the certificate is explicitly identifiable in the invoice customer block on page 1. Minor formatting differences such as 'N1' versus 'N' are in the address portion and do not affect company-name verification under the consignee rules. Country Türkiye/Turkey matches after country normalization.

goods_description.certificate_value:
1 VOLVO FM42R RIGID TRUCK
VOLVO TRUCK CHASSIS NO:
551193
goods_description.invoice_value:
EURO 6 STANDARDS
VOLVO FM42R RIGID TRUCK
VOLVO TYPE : VTC3R MODEL YEAR :2026
FM-TRUCK 551193 / YV2XTW0A6TB551193
goods_description.motivation: No article number was identified in the certificate, so controlled text matching was applied. The identity-bearing main designation 'VOLVO FM42R RIGID TRUCK' appears explicitly in the invoice on page 1. The chassis number 551193 also appears explicitly in the invoice. Additional invoice text such as EURO 6 standards and model year does not alter product identity.

quantity.motivation: The certificate quantity field explicitly states gross weight with numeric value and unit: 'GW/7147 KG'. The invoice explicitly states 'WEIGHT: 7147 KG' on page 1. Under rule 4.4.4, difference in weight-category wording does not itself cause mismatch when numeric value and unit match and the weight clearly refers to the shipment.

origin.invoice_value:
COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: BELGIUM
...
THE GOODS ARE OF BELGIAN ORIGIN.
...
ORIGINATE IN BELGIUM
origin.motivation: The certificate origin field contains 'The European Community, Belgium'. The invoice explicitly states Belgium as origin multiple times on page 1. Belgium is an EU member state, satisfying the European Community component, and the specifically named country Belgium is also explicitly identified. No non-EU origin contradicts the certificate.

overall_assessment.decision_summary: All applicable control points are verified as MATCH against the identified document pair consisting of the original certificate of origin and the invoice. No LC exception applies. No contradiction was found between certificate and invoice for consignor, consignee, goods description, quantity, or origin.
